response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and web engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js credentials, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role on its most critical axes. The position requires native-level Japanese proficiency — a non-negotiable listed requirement — which the candidate does not possess. Beyond that hard disqualifier, the role demands deep C programming expertise for embedded UNIX/Linux kernel development and hands-on experience with L2/L3 networking security features (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), none of which appear meaningfully in his background. His C/C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments and a university thesis project, far short of the proficiency needed for security-critical embedded firmware work.
strengths: Broad polyglot engineering background including C, C++, D, Java, and multiple scripting languages, showing adaptability across paradigms
Demonstrated embedded and systems-level exposure (NI LabWindows/CVI C components, hardware robotic-arm work, Linux-based audio server)
Strong software-craftsmanship culture — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development — indicating high code quality standards
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever; native-level Japanese is an explicit, non-negotiable requirement
No demonstrable experience in embedded networking security (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, LAN-switch firmware, or Linux kernel modification)
C programming is marginal and dated; the bulk of his career is web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, MongoDB), not systems/embedded C
